name: The Judge
flavor: Look here at this little town, this candleflame in the darkness. Its very existence is an act of courage and faith. And Aratis has charged you to keep it: to settle its disputes; to chronicle its tales; to defend it from darkness and ruin. Take up your hammer, Judge. Your town needs you.

backgrounds.name: Legacy
backgrounds.text:
You are the latest in a long line of Judges—born here, apprenticed to the prior Judge, and charged with the passing of the mantle. The Chronicle is a rich repository of lore, but there's no index, so good luck finding anything.

When you *Know Things about the people or history of Stonetop*, you have advantage.

When you *spend days, weeks, or months poring over the Chronicle*, ask the GM a question, and the GM will tell you what you learn in that time.

backgrounds.name: Missionary
backgrounds.text:
You are part of a larger order of Judges, sent here to protect the flickering flame of civilization. The Chronicle is relatively new; your position in town is far from certain. Add these Judges to the Neighbors section of the steading playbook (pick 2 more).

When you *call upon the Judge of another steading for aid or information*, they are oathbound to give it. You are likewise oathbound to support them.

You have an aviary in addition to your usual choice of special possessions (go mark it now). When you *send a message via trained bird*, as is the way of the Judges of your order, the GM will tell you if and when you receive a response, and what it says.

notes: You have an aviary in addition to your usual choice of special possessions.

choices.prompt: Add these Judges to the Neighbors section of the steading playbook (pick 2 more):

backgrounds.name: Prophet
backgrounds.text:
The line of Judges was broken long ago, the Chronicle lost or fallen into ruin. Aratis has called you personally to her service through dreams, omens, and visions. Some in town resent the authority you've assumed.

When you *spend a few days communing with Aratis about a threat facing Stonetop or civilization as a whole*, roll +WIS: **on a 7+**, Aratis reveals the course of action she would have you take; **on a 10+**, you also hold 2 Sanction. While *acting on her orders*, spend 1 Sanction to add +1 to a roll you just made.

instincts.name: Ambition
instincts.text: To increase your status or influence.

instincts.name: Harmony
instincts.text: To seek a path that makes everyone happy.

instincts.name: Orthodoxy
instincts.text: To strictly adhere to rules and traditions.

instincts.name: Zeal
instincts.text: To judge quickly and without doubt.

origins.prompt: Stonetop is your home, or close enough, but where are you (or your family) from originally? Pick 1 and a name to match (or make up something similar).

options.label: Gordin's Delve
options.note: Pick a name from any list

options.label: Lygos or some other southern town

list.name: Aegis of Faith
list.text: When you *wield a shield*, it can turn away spells, magical effects, and insubstantial attacks as if they were physical blows.

list.name: Armored
list.text:
When you *carry a shield*, mark only 1 load (instead of 2). Also, you can ignore the *cumbersome* tag on any armor you wear.

If you take this move at the start of play, add an iron hauberk, bronze cuirass, or scale coat to your inventory (all are 2 load, 2 armor, *warm*, *cumbersome*).

list.name: Bear Witness
list.text: When you *speak the truth with conviction and candor*, none can doubt you. They might deny what you say, but in their hearts they recognize the truth.

list.name: Break Bread
list.text: When you *share a proper meal with someone and each of you eats their fill*, each of you recovers 1d8 (extra) HP.

list.name: Bulwark
list.text: When you *Defend*, you can spend 1 Readiness to stand fast, holding your position despite what befalls you.

list.name: Censure
list.text:
When you *first denounce an individual in your presence as an agent of chaos or anathema to civilization*, they pick 1:

- They are ashamed, and act accordingly
- They are doubtful, and hesitate, pause
- They are afraid, and seek to escape
- They are enraged, and lash out predictably (the next roll against them has advantage)

list.name: Castigate
list.text: When you *Censure someone*, your voice deals 1d4 damage to them (*near*, *loud*, ignores armor).

list.name: Chronicler of Stonetop
list.text:
When you *write up detailed session notes and share them with the other players*, hold +1 Diligence.

You can spend 1 Diligence at any time to add +1 to a roll that you or a fellow player just made.

list.name: For the Greater Good
list.text: When you *Persuade someone to act in defense of their community or civilization at large*, you have advantage.

list.name: Hound of Aratis
list.text: When you *Seek Insight*, you can always ask "What here is tainted by chaos?" for free, even on a 6-.

list.name: Like a Dog with a Bone
list.text: When you *attack something you know to be tainted by chaos*, deal +1d6 damage.

list.name: Improved Stat
list.text: Each time you take this move, increase one of your stats by 1 (to a max of +2).

list.name: Knowledge is Power
list.text: When you *roll 10+ to Know Things*, you or an ally gain advantage on the next roll to act on what you learn.

list.name: Many Hands Make Light Work
list.text: When you *jump in to help another character who just rolled*, tell us how and ask the GM what else is required or what the consequences will be. If you accept, increase your ally's roll by +1.

list.name: A Bundle of Sticks Unbroken
list.text: When you Struggle as One, you and one ally of your choice have advantage.

list.name: The Hammer and the Book
list.text:
When you *strike a thing of supernatural chaos*, roll +WIS: **on a 10+**, deal your damage and choose 1 from the list below; **on a 7-9**, deal damage and choose 1, but you also expose yourself to harm or unwanted attention.

- Deal +1d6 damage
- Ignore the thing's armor or other defenses
- Suppress one of its unnatural powers
- Force it from its host

list.name: Truth or Consequences
list.text:
When you *look into someone's eyes and gaze upon their soul*, you can ask their player, "Are you lying or hiding something from me?" and get an honest answer. If the answer is "Yes," you have advantage on your next roll against them.

When you *lie or otherwise deceive someone through words*, you have disadvantage on your next roll against them.

list.name: Binding Arbitration
list.text: When you *bear witness to someone's promise or oath*, henceforth you may ask their player if they have kept their word. They must answer honestly. The character need not be present. If *they have broken their word*, you gain advantage on all rolls against them until they admit their wrongdoing and suffer an appropriate consequence (your call).

list.name: Vision Unclouded
list.text: When you *Seek Insight*, you can always ask "What here is hidden by illusion or magic?" for free, even on a 6-.

list.name: Well-Read
list.text: When you *name the source in which you read about the matter at hand*, roll +WIS to Know Things instead of +INT.

list.name: A Mighty Rampart
list.text: When you *hold Readiness* (from Defend), you cannot be forced from your position. Also, you can spend 1 Readiness to completely ignore the effects/damage of an attack that you suffer.

list.name: Armistice
list.text: When you *approach an enemy to negotiate in good faith*, they will at least hear you out. Even the most debased and savage foe will delay violence until you've had your say.

list.name: Condemn
list.text: When you *Censure someone*, they are marked with a mystical brand that cannot be removed or hidden until you dismiss it. Any intelligent creature who sees the mark recognizes the bearer as an agent of chaos and anathema to civilization.

list.name: Proclamation
list.text: When you *Censure*, you may denounce a group or faction as long as you can clearly identify them. Apply the effects of Censure to every member of that group, regardless of distance.

list.name: Mirrorshield
list.text: When you *Defend with a shield*, you can spend 1 Readiness to intercept a magical force and redirect it to a different target (or none).

list.name: Superior Stat
list.text: Increase one of your stats by +1 (to a max of +3).

list.name: The Tower Eternal
list.text: When you *Defy Danger against magic*, treat a result of 6- as a 7-9.

possessions.prompt: Pick 1, in addition to your symbol of authority and scribe's tools.

fixed.name: Your symbol of authority
fixed.text: (pick 1): ◇◇ Black iron maul, utterly immune to all magic (*close*, *forceful*, *awkward*, +1 damage); ◇◇ Makerglass shield, etched with Aratis's symbol (*indestructible*, +1 armor, +1 Readiness on a Defend 7+); ◇ Helm set with a dark ice "jewel." Grants advantage to resist mind-affecting magic.

fixed.name: Scribe's tools
fixed.text: parchment, ink, pigments, vials, quills, ◇ a notebook, etc.

options.name: Aviary
options.text: thick gloves, bird hoods, tethers, seed, ◇ messenger birds, ◇ birdcages, etc.

options.name: Carpenter's tools
options.text: chisels, files, nails, pitch, ◇ prybars, ◇ saws, ◇◇ firkins, barrels, etc.

options.name: Engineer's tools
options.text: rulers, tapes, rods, plumb-bobs, ◇ tripods, ◇ block & tackles, wheelbarrow, etc.

options.name: Smithy
options.text: (or access to it): iron goods, ingots, thick gloves, ◇ tongs, ◇ bellows, an anvil, etc.

extras.title: The Chronicle
extras.text: The Judge of Aratis is charged with maintaining the Chronicle, a history of the community, its people, their knowledge, and their traditions. The nature of the lore contained in the Chronicle depends on your Background, but it is more than a mere book; it is a physical place. Decide on its physical structure. Mark the location of the Chronicle on the Stonetop Playbook map.

options.label: … is a sturdy vault from the time of the Makers.
… has plenty of room to grow.
… has but one entrance, magically sealed.
… bears minor magics to preserve its contents.
… is warded against spirits and magic.
… sits on the outskirts, near the Old Wall.
… is little more than a crude cellar.

extras.title: The Lawkeeper
extras.text: Her Judges say that Aratis has been with humanity since they first stacked one stone upon another and called it home.

choices.prompt: In Stonetop's Pavillion of the Gods, Aratis's shrine is… (pick 1)

options.label: … a hub of the community, a place of frequent rites, petitions, and celebrations
… used only on high holidays, for each home keeps its own shrine above the hearth
… neglected by most, tended only by you and a handful of believers
… a grim place of judgement and punishment, shunned by all but her chosen

choices.prompt: Of her true disciples, Aratis demands… (choose 3)

options.label: … hospitality, freely given to all who ask for it
… adherence to strict rules of diet and dress
… respect for authority, property, and rank

introductions.intro: Wait here for everyone else. When everyone's ready, take turns introducing your characters. When *someone reveals something and you want to know more*, ask them about it. When *someone asks you a question*, answer it truthfully.

steps.text: On your first turn, **introduce yourself** by name, pronouns, background, origin, and appearance.
On your second turn, **describe your special possessions** and how you contribute to the village (beyond working the fields).
On your third turn, *describe the Chronicle*. Then, *tell us about Aratis and her shrine*, and what she demands of her true disciples.
On your next turn, **answer one of the following**, naming one or more NPCs who live in Stonetop.
questions: Who is the wisest of the town elders?

steps.text: Go around again. Answer another question from 4, or pass. When everyone has passed, go on.
On your next turn, **ask your fellow PCs one of these**. When others ask you, answer as you like.
questions: Which one of you is a true disciple of Aratis?
Which one of you is my closest confidant?
Which one of you has stood beside me in battle against unnatural chaos?
Against which of you have I passed judgement?

steps.text: Go around again. Ask another question from 6, or pass. When everyone has passed, go on.
Add your home to the steading playbook. When everyone is done, let spring burst forth!
